person_name: Vettius Agorius Praetextatus, Roman senator and governor of Achaia (d. 384)
person_name_full: Vettius Agorius Praetextatus, Roman senator and governor of Achaia (d. 384)
notes: Roman senator and governor of Achaia, noted for noble and old-fashioned dignity, active during the reign of Julian.

docs.title: BOOK XXII
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … was present, a senator of a noble disposition and of old-fashioned dignity; who at that time had come to Constantinople on his own private affairs, and whom Julian by his own choice selected as governor of Achaia with the rank of proconsul. 7.
